channel.title: Unix/Linux on cafeTheatre
channel.description: Recent content in Unix/Linux on cafeTheatre

item.title: Set up a local ubuntu mirror
item.description: Configuring apt-mirror First of all, you need to execute apt-get install apt-mirror command to install apt-mirror. The default config is just fit your server OS version, but still you can modify it to deb-amd64 | deb [arch=amd64] or deb-i386 | deb [arch=i386] in /etc/apt/mirror.
